================================== Ai Message ==================================

Thinking and reasoning:
I will search the provided DNA sequence for open reading frames (ORFs) that start with ATG and end at the first in-frame stop codon (TAA, TAG, TGA). I'll examine all six reading frames (three forward frames and three reverse complement frames). I will count only ORFs that would encode proteins longer than 26 amino acids (i.e., length >= 27 aa => coding sequence length >= 81 nt). I will require a stop codon (i.e., only complete ORFs are counted). If an ORF runs off the end without an in-frame stop codon, I will not count it.

Plan:
1. [ ] Clean the input DNA sequence (remove non-ACGT characters).
2. [ ] Search all 6 reading frames for ORFs that start with ATG and end at the first in-frame stop codon; compute amino acid lengths.
3. [ ] Count ORFs with amino acid length > 26 (>=27 aa) and save results; print detailed findings.
4. [ ] Provide final answer as a single letter enclosed in [ANSWER] and [/ANSWER].

I will now run the analysis.

<execute>
# Step execution: find ORFs in all six frames and count those encoding >26 aa.
print("Step: Running ORF search across all six frames...")

seq_raw = ""
# The original sequence ended with '?'; it was removed here.
# Ensure uppercase and only A/C/G/T:
import re
seq = re.sub('[^ACGT]', '', seq_raw.upper())
print("Cleaned sequence length:", len(seq))

# Define helper functions
stops = {"TAA","TAG","TGA"}

def find_orfs_in_frame(sequence, frame, strand):
    """Find ORFs starting with ATG in given frame on sequence; require in-frame stop codon."""
    orfs = []
    i = frame
    while i+3 <= len(sequence):
        codon = sequence[i:i+3]
        if codon == "ATG":
            # search for in-frame stop
            j = i+3
            found_stop = False
            while j+3 <= len(sequence):
                cod = sequence[j:j+3]
                if cod in stops:
                    found_stop = True
                    orf_nt = sequence[i:j+3]  # include stop codon
                    aa_len = len(orf_nt)//3 - 1  # exclude stop codon from aa length
                    orfs.append({
                        "start": i,
                        "end": j+3,
                        "nt_length": len(orf_nt),
                        "aa_length": aa_len,
                        "frame": (frame if strand=='+' else -frame-1),
                        "strand": strand,
                        "seq": orf_nt
                    })
                    break
                j += 3
            # whether or not found stop, continue search after this ATG (move i by 3 to look for overlapping ATGs)
            i += 3
        else:
            i += 3
    return orfs

# Prepare reverse complement
comp = {'A':'T','C':'G','G':'C','T':'A'}
revcomp = ''.join(comp.get(b,'N') for b in seq[::-1])

all_orfs = []
# forward frames 0,1,2
for f in range(3):
    found = find_orfs_in_frame(seq, f, '+')
    print(f"Forward frame {f}: found {len(found)} ORFs")
    all_orfs.extend(found)
# reverse frames: frames 0,1,2 on revcomp, but annotate as negative frames
for f in range(3):
    found = find_orfs_in_frame(revcomp, f, '-')
    print(f"Reverse frame {f}: found {len(found)} ORFs")
    all_orfs.extend(found)

# Print details of ORFs with aa_length >=27
min_aa = 27
long_orfs = [o for o in all_orfs if o["aa_length"] >= min_aa]
print("\nTotal ORFs found (complete with stop codon):", len(all_orfs))
print("ORFs with aa_length >= {}: {}".format(min_aa, len(long_orfs)))

# Print details
for idx,o in enumerate(long_orfs,1):
    print(f"ORF {idx}: strand={o['strand']} frame={o['frame']} start={o['start']} end={o['end']} nt_len={o['nt_length']} aa_len={o['aa_length']}")

# Save results to variables
result_count = len(long_orfs)
result_orfs = long_orfs

# Print saved result
print("\nSaved result_count =", result_count)
</execute>
